title: [lichen_l2/lora_l2] enable true path races with concurrent disable
description:
Cross-file race condition: lichen_l2_enable(true) has a TOCTOU race with concurrent lichen_l2_enable(false).

Race scenario:
1. Thread A calls lichen_l2_enable(true)
2. Thread A acquires tx_mutex then rx_mutex (lines 695-696)
3. Thread A reinitializes link_ctx, sets link_ctx_initialized=1 (line 717)
4. Thread A releases both mutexes (lines 720-721)
5. Thread B calls lichen_l2_enable(false) - acquires mutexes, sets link_ctx_initialized=0, calls cleanup
6. Thread A continues at line 728 - re-registers RX callback
7. Thread A calls lichen_lora_l2_start()
8. RX thread starts with link_ctx_initialized=0

The problem: Thread A's enable path releases mutexes between initializing link_ctx (lines 695-721) and starting the RX thread (line 729). A concurrent disable can sneak in and undo the initialization.

The start() call will succeed (it doesn't check link_ctx_initialized), but the RX thread will drop all frames at line 1109 ('RX before link_ctx initialized').

Location: lichen_l2.c:673-748

Impact: Silent packet loss - interface appears to start successfully but RX doesn't work.
close_reason: Fixed: state machine verifies STOPPED->RUNNING transition atomically
